hypothesis: If a multimodal contrastive model is trained to align SMILES strings with 3D electron-density voxel grids, then the resulting 3D molecular embeddings will be invariant to arbitrary rigid rotations—yielding higher rotated and unrotated retrieval accuracy, increased multi-pose retrieval rates, and improved pose-robust linear-probe property prediction and clustering (lower Davies–Bouldin index)—compared to unimodal 3D or SMILES-only baselines, because the orientation-agnostic SMILES signal supervises the voxel encoder to disentangle pose from molecular identity.
